title: Wing Box Load-Case Review
description: Build a simplified structural model of a wing box, apply representative flight loads, and identify the critical stress and deflection drivers.
whyItMatters: Aircraft performance is meaningless if the structure cannot carry the loads. This project introduces the structural logic behind spars, skins, load paths, and margin thinking.

steps.title: Define the structure and load case
steps.description: Create a simplified wing-box geometry and choose a representative manoeuvre or gust load case.
steps.tips: Start with a simple beam or shell representation before adding complexity.

steps.title: Estimate distributed loads
steps.description: Translate aerodynamic lift, fuel weight, and structural weight into a bending and shear picture across the span.
steps.tips: A clean free-body diagram usually prevents later modelling mistakes.

steps.title: Run the structural model
steps.description: Use an FEA tool or spreadsheet beam model to calculate stresses, deflections, and likely hot spots.
steps.tips: Check whether the answer is physically plausible before trusting the software.

steps.title: Interpret the weak points
steps.description: Identify which assumptions, material choices, or geometry features most strongly influence margin.
steps.tips: Do not only report max stress. Explain why it appears there.

steps.title: Recommend the next design change
steps.description: Propose a realistic improvement such as spar-cap resizing, skin-thickness change, or load redistribution.
steps.tips: Tie the recommendation to both structural benefit and mass penalty.

deliverables: Stress and deflection summary plots or tables
Short design note with one recommended improvement

rubric.description: The model reflects a believable load path and boundary condition set.
Results are interpreted correctly and linked back to the load case.
The recommendation acknowledges weight, manufacturability, or fatigue implications.
The structural story is clear enough for another engineer to review.
